title: WikiEditReceiverContext — Implementation Plan
required_behaviors: new: Construct WikiEditReceiverContext with shared event_sink (Arc<EventBufferContext>), stream_url, geodata_api_url, reconnect_delay, include_bot_edits; initialise empty coordinate cache; do not open SSE connection.

start: Open SSE connection to stream_url; loop reading events; call on_event_received for each SSE message; call on_stream_disconnected on any disconnection or server close; run continuously in the background (spawned thread or async task).

on_event_received: Parse raw SSE data field as JSON WikiEdit; filter by wiki pattern (*wiki but not wikimedia); filter namespace != 0; filter bot edits when include_bot_edits is false; call resolve_coordinates; if Some, construct PositionEvent(lat, lon, edit.timestamp(), 'wiki', Some(title)) and call event_sink.receive_event.

resolve_coordinates: Check coordinate cache by (wiki, title) key; if hit and not expired return cached value; otherwise call GeoData API ({wiki_base}/w/api.php?action=query&titles={title}&prop=coordinates&format=json); on success cache and return Some(lat, lon); on no-coordinates cache negative sentinel and return None; on HTTP failure return None without caching.
on_stream_disconnected: Sleep reconnect_delay; then call start; do not discard event_sink, cache, or configuration.
wiki identifier to base URL: transform 'enwiki' -> 'https://en.wikipedia.org' via string manipulation (strip 'wiki' suffix, prepend 'https://', append '.wikipedia.org').

Coordinate cache: keyed by (wiki, title); entries hold either Some(lat, lon) or negative sentinel; each entry has an expiry instant (default TTL several hours, e.g., 4 hours); expired entries are treated as missing.
event_sink abstraction: event_sink is held as Arc<EventBufferContext>; receiver calls event_sink.receive_event(event) directly; downstream code is not changed.
cross_component_integrations: WikiEditReceiverContext constructs PositionEvent with source='wiki' using PositionEvent::new
WikiEditReceiverContext parses WikiEdit from raw SSE JSON payload
WikiEditReceiverContext issues HTTP GET to GeoData API per unique (wiki, title) pair
AggregationContext shares the same Arc<EventBufferContext> instance — must not replace or clone the buffer

identity_and_sharing_constraints.subject: event_sink (EventBufferContext)
identity_and_sharing_constraints.identity_semantics: Shared identity — the same EventBufferContext instance is held by WikiEditReceiverContext, AggregationContext, and any other active receivers. Must never be replaced, re-created, or independently owned.
identity_and_sharing_constraints.mutation_semantics: EventBufferContext is internally mutable via its own Mutex-guarded event store. The Arc wrapper must not be cloned into a new allocation; only the Arc reference count increases.
identity_and_sharing_constraints.rust_guidance: Store as Arc<EventBufferContext>. Accept Arc<EventBufferContext> in new(). Do not clone the inner value; clone only the Arc to increment the reference count. Fulfils shared-reference constraint without transferring sole ownership.

identity_and_sharing_constraints.subject: coordinate_cache
identity_and_sharing_constraints.identity_semantics: Owned by WikiEditReceiverContext; not shared externally.
identity_and_sharing_constraints.mutation_semantics: Mutable — entries are added and expiry-checked on every resolve_coordinates call. Must be protected for concurrent access if start spawns a thread.
identity_and_sharing_constraints.rust_guidance: Use a Mutex<HashMap<(String, String), CacheEntry>> where CacheEntry holds an enum (Coordinates { lat: f64, lon: f64 } | Absent) plus an Instant expiry. Wrap the whole struct in Mutex or use an interior-mutable map.

identity_and_sharing_constraints.subject: WikiEditReceiverContext (self in start/on_stream_disconnected)
identity_and_sharing_constraints.identity_semantics: The context itself must persist across reconnections; on_stream_disconnected must not replace state.
identity_and_sharing_constraints.mutation_semantics: Mutable interior state (cache); immutable config (stream_url, geodata_api_url, reconnect_delay, include_bot_edits).
identity_and_sharing_constraints.rust_guidance: Wrap Self in Arc so that start can spawn a background thread holding a clone of the Arc, enabling on_stream_disconnected to call start again on the same instance without consuming ownership.

ordered_tasks.title: Define CacheEntry and CoordinateCache types
ordered_tasks.detail: Define a private enum CacheEntry { Found { lat: f64, lon: f64, expires_at: Instant }, Absent { expires_at: Instant } }. Define a type alias CoordinateCache = HashMap<(String, String), CacheEntry>. Define a constant CACHE_TTL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(4 * 3600). These types are private to the module. The Instant-based expiry allows resolve_coordinates to compare Instant::now() against expires_at.
verification_targets: CacheEntry enum has Found and Absent variants each with expires_at
CACHE_TTL constant is defined as several hours

ordered_tasks.title: Define WikiEditReceiverContext struct
ordered_tasks.detail: Define the public struct WikiEditReceiverContext with fields: event_sink: Arc<EventBufferContext>, stream_url: String, geodata_api_url: String, reconnect_delay: Duration, include_bot_edits: bool, coordinate_cache: Mutex<CoordinateCache>. Derive Debug where possible. The struct is designed to be wrapped in Arc<Self> by the caller so that start can spawn a background task referencing the same instance.
verification_targets: struct fields match all props from specification
event_sink field is Arc<EventBufferContext>
coordinate_cache field is Mutex-guarded

ordered_tasks.title: Implement new()
ordered_tasks.detail: Implement WikiEditReceiverContext::new(event_sink: Arc<EventBufferContext>, stream_url: String, geodata_api_url: String, reconnect_delay: Duration, include_bot_edits: bool) -> Self. Store all arguments into the corresponding struct fields. Initialise coordinate_cache as Mutex::new(HashMap::new()). Do not open any connection. Log construction with tracing::info.
verification_targets: new() does not open SSE connection
new() initialises empty coordinate cache
new() accepts Arc<EventBufferContext> without cloning the inner value

ordered_tasks.title: Implement wiki-to-base-URL helper
ordered_tasks.detail: Implement a private fn wiki_to_base_url(wiki: &str) -> String. Strategy: strip trailing 'wiki' from the identifier (e.g., 'enwiki' -> 'en'), prepend 'https://', append '.wikipedia.org'. Example: 'enwiki' -> 'https://en.wikipedia.org'. Edge cases: 'commonswiki' -> strip 'wiki' -> 'commons' -> 'https://commons.wikipedia.org' (acceptable). This is a pure string transformation. Log the mapping at debug level.
verification_targets: enwiki maps to https://en.wikipedia.org
dewiki maps to https://de.wikipedia.org

ordered_tasks.title: Implement resolve_coordinates()
ordered_tasks.detail: Implement fn resolve_coordinates(&self, wiki: &str, title: &str) -> Option<(f64, f64)>. Steps: (1) Lock coordinate_cache; check for key (wiki, title); if entry exists and expires_at > Instant::now(), return Some((lat, lon)) for Found or None for Absent; release lock. (2) If no valid cache entry, drop lock and issue HTTP GET to {wiki_base}/w/api.php?action=query&titles={title}&prop=coordinates&format=json using a blocking HTTP client (e.g., ureq) or async client (e.g., reqwest) consistent with the execution model chosen in start. (3) Parse JSON response: if coordinates array is non-empty, extract lat/lon, re-acquire lock, insert CacheEntry::Found { lat, lon, expires_at: Instant::now() + CACHE_TTL }, release lock, return Some((lat, lon)). (4) If coordinates array is empty or missing, re-acquire lock, insert CacheEntry::Absent { expires_at: Instant::now() + CACHE_TTL }, return None. (5) If HTTP call fails (network error, parse error), return None without writing to cache. Log each branch at debug level.
verification_targets: Cache hit for Found returns coordinates without HTTP call
Cache hit for Absent returns None without HTTP call
Expired cache entry triggers new HTTP call
HTTP failure returns None and does not write to cache
Successful HTTP response with no coordinates stores Absent sentinel

ordered_tasks.title: Implement on_event_received()
ordered_tasks.detail: Implement fn on_event_received(&self, raw_event: &str). Steps: (1) Parse raw_event as serde_json::Value; extract 'data' field if present (SSE wraps payload in 'data'), or parse raw_event directly as JSON. (2) Check 'type' field — accept only 'edit' or 'new'; otherwise return early. (3) Extract wiki, namespace (i32), title, timestamp (Unix epoch i64 -> DateTime<Utc> via DateTime::from_timestamp), bot (bool), user fields. Construct WikiEdit via WikiEdit::new(...). (4) Filter wiki: the wiki field must end with 'wiki' but must not equal 'wikimedia'; if it fails this check, return early. Concretely: wiki.ends_with("wiki") && wiki != "wikimedia". (5) If namespace != 0, return early. (6) If !include_bot_edits && wiki_edit.bot(), return early. (7) Call self.resolve_coordinates(wiki_edit.wiki(), wiki_edit.title()). (8) If Some((lat, lon)), construct PositionEvent::new(lat, lon, wiki_edit.timestamp(), EventSource::Wiki (or the string 'wiki' per PositionEvent's source type), Some(wiki_edit.title().to_string())) and call self.event_sink.receive_event(event). Log each discard path at debug level.
verification_targets: Edit to enwiki article namespace=0 with coordinates produces PositionEvent delivered to event_sink
Edit with namespace != 0 is discarded
Bot edit with include_bot_edits=false is discarded
Edit to wikimedia project is discarded
Edit with no GeoData coordinates produces no PositionEvent
PositionEvent has source='wiki', label=title, occurred_at from edit timestamp

ordered_tasks.title: Implement on_stream_disconnected()
ordered_tasks.detail: Implement fn on_stream_disconnected(self: Arc<Self>). Steps: (1) Log the disconnection at info level. (2) Sleep for self.reconnect_delay (use std::thread::sleep or async equivalent consistent with start's execution model). (3) Call self.start() to reconnect. Does not discard event_sink, coordinate_cache, or any configuration field. The Arc<Self> parameter ensures the instance remains alive through the sleep and the subsequent start call.
verification_targets: on_stream_disconnected sleeps for reconnect_delay before calling start
event_sink, cache, and config remain intact after reconnection
start is called on the same Arc<Self> instance

ordered_tasks.title: Implement start()
ordered_tasks.detail: Implement pub fn start(self: Arc<Self>). Steps: (1) Spawn a background thread (std::thread::spawn) holding a clone of Arc<Self>. Inside the thread: (2) Open an SSE connection to self.stream_url using an SSE/HTTP client (e.g., eventsource-client crate or manual HTTP chunked-read). (3) Loop: for each SSE event received, call self.on_event_received(raw_data). (4) On any connection error or stream end, break the inner loop and call self.on_stream_disconnected() (which will sleep and restart). This provides automatic reconnect. Log stream open and errors at info level. The outer start method returns immediately after spawning; all blocking work happens inside the thread.
verification_targets: start returns immediately (non-blocking to caller)
background thread reads SSE events continuously
on_stream_disconnected is called when the stream drops
reconnect loop re-calls start on the same Arc<Self>

ordered_tasks.title: Wire imports, Cargo dependencies, and module registration
ordered_tasks.detail: Add required Cargo.toml dependencies: serde and serde_json for JSON parsing; chrono for DateTime<Utc>; a blocking HTTP client (ureq) or async (reqwest) for GeoData API calls; an SSE client crate (e.g., eventsource-client or es-client) for the stream connection; tracing for logging. Register the new module in src/contexts/mod.rs (pub mod wiki_edit_receiver_context). Add use statements at the top of the file: Arc, Mutex, Duration, Instant from std; DateTime<Utc> from chrono; EventBufferContext, PositionEvent, WikiEdit from their respective modules.
verification_targets: Module compiles without missing imports
EventBufferContext is imported from src/contexts/event_buffer_context.rs
PositionEvent is imported from src/data/position_event.rs
WikiEdit is imported from src/data/wiki_edit.rs

ordered_tasks.title: Verify all acceptance scenarios
ordered_tasks.detail: Trace through each acceptance example from the specification against the implementation: (1) Edit to Eiffel Tower on enwiki with GeoData coordinates -> PositionEvent at Paris coords delivered to event_sink. (2) Edit to namespace!=0 page -> silently discarded. (3) Bot edit with include_bot_edits=false -> silently discarded. (4) Edit with no GeoData coordinates -> no event, negative cache entry stored. (5) Same article edited twice quickly -> second edit uses cache, no HTTP call. (6) SSE stream disconnects -> reconnect after reconnect_delay. Confirm that EventBufferContext.receive_event is called with correct PositionEvent fields in scenario 1. Confirm cache expiry logic works for scenario 5.
verification_targets: Acceptance scenario 1: PositionEvent delivered with correct lat/lon/source/label
Acceptance scenario 2: namespace filter discards event
Acceptance scenario 3: bot filter discards event
Acceptance scenario 4: negative cache entry prevents re-query
Acceptance scenario 5: cache hit prevents second HTTP call
Acceptance scenario 6: reconnect after stream drop
functionality: new(event_sink, stream_url, geodata_api_url, reconnect_delay, include_bot_edits)
functionality: start
functionality: on_event_received(raw_event)
functionality: resolve_coordinates(wiki, title)
functionality: on_stream_disconnected
role_method: event_sink.receive_event(event)
call_edge: WikiEditReceiverContext (EventSource) -> EventBufferContext
call_edge: event_sink -> EventBufferContext (shared Arc identity preserved)
call_edge: resolve_coordinates -> Option<(f64, f64)> (Some/None)
constraint: event_sink is Arc<EventBufferContext> — same instance shared with AggregationContext
constraint: coordinate cache survives reconnection
constraint: wiki filter rejects non-Wikipedia projects and wikimedia
constraint: namespace filter rejects non-article pages
constraint: bot filter rejects bot edits when include_bot_edits=false
constraint: cache negative sentinel prevents re-querying absent coordinates
constraint: HTTP failure does not populate cache (allows retry)

risks.risk: SSE client library availability: the Rust SSE ecosystem is fragmented. If eventsource-client is unavailable or incompatible, a manual HTTP chunked-read implementation may be needed.
risks.mitigation: Prefer eventsource-client or reqwest with manual SSE line parsing as a fallback. Keep SSE reading isolated behind a private helper so the library can be swapped.

risks.risk: Concurrency between the background SSE thread and coordinate_cache access: the Mutex must not be held across an HTTP call or it will block all other threads.
risks.mitigation: Always release the Mutex lock before issuing the HTTP request; re-acquire it only to write the result. Use a local variable to hold the result between the unlock and the re-lock.

risks.risk: wiki filter regex: the pattern '*wiki' (ends with 'wiki') must exclude 'wikimedia'. A naive ends_with('wiki') check passes 'wikimedia' because 'wikimedia' ends in 'ia' not 'wiki'. However, 'wikimedia' does NOT end with 'wiki', so ends_with('wiki') is sufficient. Verify: 'enwiki'.ends_with('wiki') == true, 'wikimedia'.ends_with('wiki') == false. The spec note 'or matches wikimedia' is a clarification of corner cases in the glob pattern, not an additional exclusion needed.
risks.mitigation: Implement as: wiki.ends_with('wiki') && wiki != 'wikimedia'. Add a unit test for 'wikimedia' and 'enwiki' inputs.

risks.risk: Timestamp parsing: the SSE stream carries Unix epoch integers (seconds), but WikiEdit.timestamp is DateTime<Utc>. The on_event_received implementation must convert correctly using DateTime::from_timestamp(seconds, 0).
risks.mitigation: Use chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp or DateTime::from_utc(NaiveDateTime::from_timestamp(seconds, 0), Utc). Add a unit test covering epoch conversion.

forbidden_regressions: Do not remove or replace Arc<EventBufferContext> shared identity — the same buffer instance must remain accessible to AggregationContext and any other receivers.
Do not call EventBufferContext::new inside WikiEditReceiverContext — it must accept and hold the existing Arc<EventBufferContext>.
Do not discard the coordinate_cache on reconnection — on_stream_disconnected must preserve the cache across reconnects.
Do not allow receive_event to be called for events that fail any of the three filters (wiki project, namespace, bot).
Do not store a cache entry on HTTP failure — only failed API calls must be retryable.
Do not expose WikiEdit outside of WikiEditReceiverContext — it is a boundary type confined to this module.
Do not set PositionEvent.source to any value other than 'wiki' in on_event_received.
